title: None of the Above: Protest Voting in the World's Largest Democracy
journal: Journal of the European Economic Association
abstract: Abstract Who are “protest voters” and do they affect elections? We study this question using the introduction of a pure protest option (“None Of The Above” (NOTA)) on Indian ballots. Using structural estimation, we find that in elections without NOTA, most protest voters simply abstain. Protest voters who turn out scatter their votes among many candidates and consequently have little impact on election results. From a policy perspective, NOTA may be an effective tool to increase political participation, and can attenuate the electoral impact of compulsory voting.
summary_en: In 2013, India introduced a "None of the Above" (NOTA) option on ballots — a pure protest vote. This paper uses the reform to answer two questions: who are protest voters, and do they matter? Structural estimation shows that without a NOTA option, most protest voters simply abstain. Those who do turn out spread their votes thinly across many candidates and have almost no impact on who wins. From a policy standpoint, NOTA is an effective tool for raising turnout (including among dissatisfied voters) and could ease some of the distortions created by compulsory voting in other countries.
data_used: Structural model estimated on Indian parliamentary and state election data pre- and post-2013 NOTA reform. Voting records, candidate shares, turnout, and the NOTA share.
policy_relevance: Directly relevant to Hungarian debates on ballot design and voter engagement. Introducing a NOTA option would likely raise engagement among dissatisfied voters without distorting outcomes. Relevant for NVB (National Election Office) and for legislative debates on ballot design. Caveat: India's political system differs markedly from Hungary's (many candidates, first-past-the-post). Magnitudes and the turnout effect in a Hungarian mixed system would be different; a calibration to Hungarian polling data would be a useful next step.
policy_instruments: ballot design (None of the Above option)
